examples.title: Kosaraju's Algorithm
examples.description: An algorithm used to find strongly connected components in a graph.
examples.code:
ALGORITHM Kosaraju's Algorithm
BEGIN
  Initialize an empty stack
  Perform a depth-first search on the graph and push each vertex onto the stack
  Initialize an empty list to store the strongly connected components
  While the stack is not empty
    Pop a vertex from the stack and add it to the list of strongly connected components
    Perform a depth-first search on the graph starting from the popped vertex
  END WHILE
END
examples.explanation: Kosaraju's algorithm is used to find strongly connected components in a graph. It works by performing a depth-first search on the graph and pushing each vertex onto a stack. Then, it pops each vertex from the stack and adds it to a list of strongly connected components. Finally, it performs a depth-first search on the graph starting from each vertex in the list of strongly connected components.

examples.title: Tarjan's Algorithm
examples.description: An algorithm used to find strongly connected components in a graph.
examples.explanation: Tarjan's algorithm is used to find strongly connected components in a graph. It works by performing a depth-first search on the graph and keeping track of the lowlinks and index of each vertex. When a vertex is found to be the root of a strongly connected component, the algorithm pops all the vertices in the component from the stack and adds them to the result list.
